Author: anfroholic
Content:
@RebelMike the 1xp5 boards have arrived. Pretty sure some will be bonded shortly. 
@Tim Edwards I think yours and @Greg will also be bonded in this run.

Author: chasees18
Content: Hi Andrew @Andrew Wingate and Tim @Tim 'mithro' Ansell , I have heard from Tim that there is a good method verifying the wire bond connections. Would you mind helping pointing me to the correct location where this verification method is discussed. This would help a lot in our chip debugging. Many thanks.

Author: rebelmike
Content: Was the ground fill on the region between the die and the pins intentional?  I think that was blocked originally.  And there was some discussion of putting soldermask in that region so the vias were covered, is that still something that we think we should try?

Author: namibj
Content: Oh as I see this now if they're done by JLCPCB, I'd propose using their serialization service that'll give per-individual silkscreen serials in plain text (+ (QR/datamatrix)), though for proper traceability beyond the bondhouse the die sorter would probably have to place the dies directly onto the COB and electronically record what specific die got placed onto that serial number COB?

Author: namibj
Content: Iirc we'd need those vias covered at the top or fully open to prevent air bubbles inside from going up through the encapsulation.

Author: namibj
Content: ESD diode of the pad can be easily checked for presence once you have a working GND and a working VDD pad.

Author: namibj
Content: Iirc you just carefully diode test with pad voltage below GND to see the diode to GND, and above VDD to see the diode to VDD.

Author: chasees18
Content: Thanks for the suggestion it’s good idea to check the bonding by testing the ESD diode. I am not sure the method mentioned by Tim is same as this, or there are other method verifying it (physically or electrically)

Author: namibj
Content: AFAIK the esd diode was the suggested check of bonding, shy of any testability instrumentation in the chip itself.
